message:
fix(markdown): improve streaming fragment link completion

Refactor the complete_fragment() function that handles incomplete markdown
during streaming/incremental rendering (e.g., LLM token-by-token output).

The previous implementation had issues with nested brackets and parentheses
in links like `[text [nested]](url(with(parens)))`.

Changes:
- Replace simple counter-based tracking with explicit state machine
- States: None → InBracket → AfterBracket → InParen → None
- Track bracket_depth and paren_depth separately
- Correctly handle:
  * Nested brackets inside link text [text [nested] more]
  * Parentheses in URLs (common in Wikipedia links)
  * Abandoned links (bracket followed by non-paren)
  * Multiple sequential links

The state machine ensures:
- InBracket state: unclosed [text... completes as [text](...)
- AfterBracket state: [text]... (no paren yet) completes as [text](...)
- InParen state: [text](url... completes as [text](url)

Also includes minor formatting cleanup (rustfmt adjustments).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
